The [[Standard year/Legends|year]] '''19,997 before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] ([[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]])''' was a span of [[Time/Legends|time]] in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Great Manifest Period/Legends|Great Manifest Period]], an era of expansion by the Republic into the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. Around this time, the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Neona]] underwent a substantial climate change that caused the melting of all of the planet's surface [[Water/Legends|ice]]. The rising waters marked the extinction of the [[Unidentified Neona species|original planetary natives]] of Neona.

==Proem==
The [[Expansionist Period/Legends|Expansionist Era]] of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] history closed [[20,000 BBY/Legends|three years earlier]]. The new era, later known as the [[Great Manifest Period/Legends|Great Manifest Period]], was an era of expansion and exploration beyond the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and the then-known [[galactic community]] that was nestled along the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]] and the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]]. The exploration was begun in the then-termed "[[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]" area that later became known as the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. The settlement of these new [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] was sponsored by both the Republic government and the corporate infrastructure. Companies and prosperous planets, such as [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], pushed the limits of settlement ever Rimward.<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==The galaxy in 19,997 BBY==
19,997 BBY was a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] in the early Great Manifest Period, during which companies and planetary governments pushed the limits of settlement further toward the rim.<ref name="EA" /> Around this time in the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]],<ref name="LotE" /> although unknown to the broader Republic,<ref name="EA" /> the planet [[Neona]] underwent a significant climatic shift. The [[Water/Legends|ice]] caps on the planet melted and began flooding the surface. As the waters rose, all available surface land for non-aquatic [[Species/Legends|species]] became submerged. The [[Unidentified Neona species|native sentient species]] became extinct through this event.<ref name="LotE" />

==Impact==
The Galactic Republic saw massive expansion into the Expansion Region and broader areas of the Slice as the Great Manifest Period continued. Planets such as Coruscant and [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] were among the most active and prosperous, striking the ire of rival world [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]]. This was a principal motivation behind the [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|Alsakan Conflicts]] almost [[17,018 BBY|three thousand years later]]. At the same time, the corporate-sponsored settlement caused local resources and settlers to be exploited.<ref name="EA" />

In the Tapani [[Sector/Legends|sector]], Neona remained uninhabited for a time. Eventually, however, business interests discovered the rich [[Ore/Legends|ore]] deposits in the subterranean layers. As the planet was being [[Mining/Legends|mined]], the ruins of its native species was discovered among the rubble on the sea floor.<ref name="LotE" />

==Notable events==
*Neona underwent a dramatic climate change which caused the surface to become submerged underwater.<ref name="LotE" />
*The sentient species of Neona was driven to extinction as a result of the climate change.<ref name="LotE" />

==Behind the scenes==
The source ''[[Lords of the Expanse]]'' makes only a passing reference to the native species of Neona and their demise by stating that it took place approximately twenty thousand years before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the date by which the sourcebook was set.<ref name="LotE" /> While the events on Neona are not mentioned in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', this source establishes that the Tapani sector was not in contact with the Galactic Republic at the time of the species's extinction.<ref name="EA" />
